info.title: Praxrr API
info.description: API for Praxrr - Profile management and sync for *arr applications

tags.name: Entity Testing
tags.description: Release parsing and custom format evaluation

tags.name: Impact Simulator
tags.description: Configuration change impact simulation (scores, thresholds, cascade)

tags.name: Arr
tags.description: Arr instance library and release endpoints

tags.name: Trash Guide
tags.description: TRaSH Guide cached entity and source management

tags.name: PCD
tags.description: PCD entity export and portable format endpoints

tags.name: Resolved Config
tags.description: Resolved PCD configuration state, layer breakdown, and live/cross-instance diff endpoints

tags.name: Dependency Graph
tags.description: Resolved dependency relationships between PCD config entities, with per-entity impact

tags.name: User Preferences
tags.description: Durable UI preference storage for progressive disclosure

tags.name: Complexity Tiers
tags.description: Per-section complexity tier storage for progressive complexity

tags.name: Setup
tags.description: First-run setup wizard state and Arr connection testing

tags.name: Compatibility
tags.description: Cross-Arr compatibility parity map and semantic difference catalog

get.description:
Returns the health status of the application and its components.

Status values:
- `healthy`: All components functioning normally
- `degraded`: Core functionality works but some components have issues
- `unhealthy`: Core functionality is broken

Use `?verbose=true` for detailed component information.
Returns the OpenAPI specification for this API
Returns the availability status of the release title parser service.

parserAvailable.description: Whether the parser service is reachable and healthy

get.description:
Returns the latest startup pull run with per-instance outcomes.

The startup pull feature reconstructs Arr sync selections from live Arr
state on application startup when `PULL_ON_START=true`. This endpoint
provides visibility into the most recent run for support and diagnostics.

Run-level status values:
- `success`: All instances completed without failures
- `partial`: Some instances succeeded and some failed
- `failed`: All instances failed
- `skipped`: No instances were processed
- `disabled`: Feature was disabled at run time

Per-instance status values follow `JobRunStatus`:
- `success`: Instance pull completed
- `failure`: Instance pull failed
- `skipped`: Instance was skipped
- `cancelled`: Instance pull was cancelled

200.description: Latest startup pull run with per-instance outcomes

500.description: Failed to fetch latest startup pull run

get.description:
Returns the preferred disclosure mode for a section key.

Missing persisted values are returned as default `basic` unless `strict`
is enabled.

parameters.name: section_key
parameters.description: Canonical section key for progressive disclosure state

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: When true, missing rows return 404 instead of default response

404.description: Preference not found for strict read

patch.description:
Creates or updates the stored disclosure mode for a section key.

Payloads include optional concurrency token `expected_updated_at`.

get.description:
Returns the preferred complexity tier and progression counters for a
section key.

Missing persisted values are returned as default `beginner` unless
`strict` is enabled.

parameters.name: section_key
parameters.description: Canonical section key for progressive complexity state

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: When true, missing rows return 404 instead of default response

404.description: Complexity tier not found for strict read

patch.description:
Creates or updates the stored complexity tier for a section key.

Payloads include optional concurrency token `expected_updated_at`.
Activity deltas are bounded and may be used to drive non-intrusive
progression suggestions.

429.description: Complexity tier updates are being rate-limited

post.description:
Parses release titles and evaluates them against all custom formats in the specified database.

This endpoint:
- Parses each release title to extract metadata (resolution, source, languages, etc.)
- Matches regex patterns using .NET-compatible regex via the parser service
- Evaluates each release against all custom formats in the database
- Returns which custom formats match each release

Results are cached for performance - repeated requests with the same titles will be faster.

post.summary: Simulate custom format scoring for releases
post.description:
Parses release titles, evaluates custom format matches, and calculates
per-profile scores for interactive score simulation.

post.summary: Simulate configuration change impact for releases
post.description:
Applies proposed custom-format score and quality-profile threshold
changes to an isolated ephemeral sandbox cache and returns release-level
impact, config A/B diff, and cascade warnings. Proposed changes never
touch pcd_ops or the registered cache.

400.description: Invalid request (bad JSON, cap violation, bad arrType, unknown change kind)

404.description: Database instance or built cache not found

get.description:
Fetches the full library from an Arr instance with quality profile,
score, and progress information.

- For Radarr: Returns movies with file quality, custom format scores, and cutoff progress
- For Sonarr: Returns series with season summaries and episode statistics
- For Lidarr: Returns artists/albums (capability-gated; detailed payload fields deferred to later phases)

Results include Praxrr profile matching to indicate which items use managed profiles.
Responses are cached server-side for 5 minutes.
Supports server-side filtering, sorting, and pagination.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of items per page. Values above 250 are capped at 250.

parameters.name: sortKey
parameters.description:
Optional sort field. Must be valid for the target Arr type
(radarr, sonarr, or lidarr) or request will be rejected.

parameters.name: sortDirection
parameters.description: Sort order for the sortKey field.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive text filter applied before sorting and pagination.

200.description: Library items with quality and score data

400.description: Invalid instanceId, page, or sort/query parameters

delete.description:
Clears the server-side cache for an Arr instance's library data.
The next GET request will fetch fresh data from the Arr instance.

get.summary: Get episode details for a Sonarr series
get.description:
Lazy-loads episode-level data for a specific series in a Sonarr instance.
Returns episodes with quality, custom format scores, and cutoff progress.

This endpoint is designed for on-demand loading when a series row is expanded
in the library view. Sonarr instances only.

Results are cached server-side for 5 minutes per series.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.description: Arr instance ID (must be a Sonarr instance)

200.description: Episode details with quality and score data

400.description: Invalid parameters or not a Sonarr instance

get.description:
Triggers an interactive search on an Arr instance and returns grouped/deduplicated results.

For Radarr: Searches for releases for the specified movie.
For Sonarr: Searches for season pack releases for the specified series and season.
For Lidarr: Searches for releases for the specified album.

Results are grouped by title, combining information from multiple indexers.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Movie ID (Radarr), Series ID (Sonarr), or Album ID (Lidarr)

parameters.name: season
parameters.description: Season number for Sonarr searches (defaults to 1)

post.summary: Scan or execute cleanup of stale configs
post.description:
Detects and deletes stale namespace-suffixed custom formats and quality profiles
from an Arr instance that are no longer in the current sync selections.

Two actions are supported via the `action` field in the request body:
- `scan`: Scans for stale items and returns what would be deleted.
- `execute`: Deletes the stale items from a previous scan result.

Quality profiles assigned to media will be skipped with a warning (Arr returns HTTP 500 "in use").

get.description: Returns a single TRaSH entity from a synced source cache by trashId.

parameters.name: trashId
parameters.description: TRaSH entity ID (32 lowercase hex chars)

404.description: Source or TRaSH entity not found

500.description: Failed to read TRaSH entity cache

post.description:
Computes a read-only preview of the sync changes for an Arr instance.

This endpoint performs no writes. It only reads from PCD and Arr GET
endpoints to compare desired versus current state, then stores a preview
snapshot for later retrieval or application.

If no `sections` are provided, all configured sections for the instance are
included.

400.description: Invalid instanceId, unsupported section, or request body

409.description: Preview for this instance is already generating

429.description: Preview creation is rate-limited or preview-store capacity is exhausted

get.description:
Retrieves a previously generated preview snapshot by ID.

This endpoint is read-only and is safe to call repeatedly while preview state
changes naturally over time.

Snapshots are subject to staleness rules enforced by the preview TTL. A
preview that has exceeded the TTL should be treated as expired.

delete.description:
Removes a stored preview snapshot.

This endpoint does not trigger any Arr sync action; it only removes the cached
preview and does not change runtime configuration.

post.description:
Applies a previously generated preview to the target Arr instance.

Preview generation itself is read-only, but this operation runs the normal sync
execution path for the selected sections.

Apply rejects previews that are too stale based on policy:
- warnings are returned when the preview is older than 5 minutes
- apply is blocked when staleness exceeds the hard threshold

409.description: Preview is not in a ready state, contains failed sections, or instance is syncing

422.description: Preview is stale beyond apply threshold

get.description:
Serializes a PCD entity into its portable JSON representation.

The portable format strips database IDs and timestamps, producing a
self-contained snapshot suitable for clipboard copy, file export, or
cross-database import.

parameters.name: databaseId
parameters.description: The PCD database ID to export from

post.description:
Deserializes a portable entity into PCD operations, creating the entity
in the target database and layer.

The request body matches the export response format (`entityType` + `data`),
plus `databaseId` and `layer` to specify the target.
Optional `migration` metadata (`format`, `version`, `source`) can be provided
for hybrid JSON/YAML ingestion flows.

Name uniqueness is validated — importing an entity with a name that already
exists will return a 400 error.

400.description: Validation error (missing fields, duplicate name, invalid data)

post.description:
Creates a manual snapshot of the current PCD database state. The snapshot
captures ops metadata and a state fingerprint for future restore operations.

get.summary: List PCD snapshots for a database
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of snapshots for the specified database, ordered
by creation time descending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description:
Returns full snapshot detail including computed fields for restore context
(opsWrittenSince, isRestorable). Restore support is not yet implemented,
so `isRestorable` is always false.

404.description: Snapshot not found or doesn't belong to database

delete.description:
Deletes a snapshot. Ownership is enforced — the snapshot must belong to the
specified database.

404.description: Snapshot not found or doesn't belong to database

get.description:
Reads Lidarr metadata profiles from a PCD database.

Metadata profiles are a Lidarr-only entity family and are not available
for Radarr or Sonarr entities.

Read payloads are list DTOs that expose counts, not full type payloads.

post.description:
Creates a Lidarr metadata profile in the selected PCD database.

Runtime validation enforces Lidarr-specific rules including:
- case-insensitive unique profile name
- reserved name `None` is rejected
- all source categories include explicit `allowed` booleans
- type/status rows in write payloads use `id`, `name`, and `allowed`.

403.description: Base layer write is not allowed

get.description:
Returns a single Lidarr metadata profile by local profile id.

Payloads are the PCD contract used by runtime sync and portable
operations, not the Lidarr API payload shape.

put.description:
Updates selected fields of an existing Lidarr metadata profile.

Partial updates are supported for edit flows.
Updates remain Lidarr-only and validate value-guard constraints.

At least one mutable field must be provided.
Updates use stable-name/value-guard semantics for concurrency safety.

delete.description:
Deletes a Lidarr metadata profile by id.

Deletes are rejected if runtime guard checks detect in-use rows or stale
value guards.
This endpoint must only be invoked for Lidarr-backed databases.
Delete payloads must include the current row name for value-guard safety.

get.summary: List resolved config entities by type
get.description:
Returns resolved config state for every entity of the given type in the
PCD database, for the selected layer.

`layer` defaults to `resolved` (the schema -> base -> tweaks -> user
replay, matching what sync applies to Arr instances). `arrType` is
required for per-arr-app entity types (`naming`, `mediaSettings`,
`qualityDefinitions`) and rejected for arr-agnostic entity types
(`delayProfile`, `regularExpression`, `customFormat`, `qualityProfile`)
and for `lidarrMetadataProfile`, which is Lidarr-only and implicit.

parameters.name: arrType
parameters.description:
Arr app type. Required for per-arr-app entity types (naming,
mediaSettings, qualityDefinitions); rejected for arr-agnostic entity
types and for lidarrMetadataProfile.

400.description: Invalid databaseId, unknown entityType, missing/invalid arrType, or database not ready

500.description: Failed to read resolved config state

get.summary: Get one resolved config entity by name
get.description:
Returns resolved config state for a single named entity, for the
selected layer.

`layer` defaults to `resolved`. `404` is reserved for a hard miss in the
`resolved` layer; the `base`/`user` layers return `200` with
`present: false` when the entity does not exist in that layer.

parameters.name: arrType
parameters.description:
Arr app type. Required for per-arr-app entity types (naming,
mediaSettings, qualityDefinitions); rejected for arr-agnostic entity
types and for lidarrMetadataProfile.

400.description: Invalid databaseId, unknown entityType, missing/invalid arrType, or database not ready

404.description: Database not found, or entity not found in the resolved layer

500.description: Failed to read resolved config state

get.summary: Compare one resolved config entity across instances
get.description:
Computes per-instance transformed-desired payloads (and optionally live
Arr state) for a single named entity across up to 8 Arr instances, with
pairwise diffs against the first compatible instance.

Per-instance failures (unreachable, incompatible arr_type, unsupported
entity type) are reported as inline statuses on that instance and never
fail the whole request.

parameters.name: instanceIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated Arr instance IDs to compare (maximum 8)

parameters.name: includeLive
parameters.description:
When true, also fetch live per-instance state. Live fetches are
rate-limited per instance.

400.description: Invalid databaseId/entityType, invalid or unknown instanceIds, instance cap exceeded, or database not ready

404.description: Database not found, or entity not found

get.summary: Diff resolved config entity against live Arr state
get.description:
Computes the desired-vs-actual field diff for a single named entity on
one Arr instance, via the sync-preview section syncer filtered to the
entity (namespace-suffix aware). An empty `changes` array means the
entity is in sync; it is never conflatable with a failed check.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.description: Arr instance ID to diff resolved state against

400.description: Invalid databaseId/entityType, entity type unsupported for the instance's arr_type, or database not ready

404.description: Database not found, Arr instance not found, or entity not found

429.description: Live diff requests are being rate-limited

get.summary: Get the resolved dependency graph for a PCD database
get.description:
Returns the dependency graph of config entities for a PCD database,
computed on demand from the in-memory PCD cache (read-only). Nodes are
custom formats, regular expressions, quality profiles, qualities, and
quality definitions; edges are referrer -> dependency relationships:
quality profile -> custom format (scored), custom format -> regular
expression, quality profile -> quality, and quality definition ->
quality.

`arrType` filters to edges scoped to that Arr app (plus `all` edges) and
to quality profiles compatible with it; `nodeKind` filters nodes/edges to
a single entity family. All arr-scoped edges resolve by explicit
`arr_type` with no sibling fallback.

parameters.name: arrType
parameters.description: Restrict to edges scoped to this Arr app (and `all`) and compatible quality profiles

parameters.name: nodeKind
parameters.description: Restrict the graph to a single entity family

400.description: Invalid databaseId, unknown arrType/nodeKind, or database not ready

500.description: Failed to build the dependency graph

get.summary: Get the dependency impact of a single entity
get.description:
Returns the entities that reference (`direction=dependents`, default) or
are referenced by (`direction=dependencies`) a single named node, via a
bounded traversal over the dependency graph. Powers editor "Used by"
panels, cascade-delete warnings, and node-focus navigation.

`name` may contain reserved characters (it is a free-form entity name);
clients MUST percent-encode it. `depth` is clamped to `[1, 3]`. A
well-formed request for a name that does not exist returns `404`; an
unknown or not-yet-built `databaseId` returns `400`.

parameters.name: nodeKind
parameters.description: Entity family of the focus node

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the focus entity (percent-encoded; may contain reserved characters)

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Traverse to dependents (reverse, default) or dependencies (forward)

parameters.name: arrType
parameters.description: Restrict to edges scoped to this Arr app (and `all`)

400.description: Invalid databaseId, unknown nodeKind/arrType, or database not ready

404.description: No node with that kind and name (an unknown or not-yet-built databaseId is 400, not 404)

get.description:
Returns the current wizard step, completion/dismissal status,
prerequisite progress, and default database auto-link info used to
render and resume the setup wizard.

403.description: Setup has already been completed or dismissed

patch.description:
Advances the wizard to the given step. Rejects steps outside the known
wizard sequence.

403.description: Setup has already been completed or dismissed

post.summary: Test an Arr connection during setup
post.description:
Validates Arr connection credentials without persisting an instance.

This endpoint performs no writes. It is rate-limited per client IP and
rejects metadata/link-local URLs before attempting a connection, so it
cannot be used as an SSRF or port-scan oracle.

400.description: Missing/invalid type, url, or apiKey, or an unsafe URL

403.description: Setup has already been completed or dismissed

post.description:
Marks the setup wizard completed.

Terminal and idempotent by design: unlike `GET`/`PATCH /state`, this
endpoint does not require the wizard to still be in progress, so a
repeated call succeeds instead of rejecting once setup is already
completed.

Marks the setup wizard dismissed.

Terminal and idempotent by design: unlike `GET`/`PATCH /state`, this
endpoint does not require the wizard to still be in progress, so a
repeated call succeeds instead of rejecting once setup is already
dismissed.

get.description:
Returns the static cross-Arr entity/app support matrix and catalog of known
semantic differences between Arr apps.

When `databaseId` is provided, also computes per-profile compatibility for
that database's quality profiles against each Arr app's compatible quality
names, attached as `profiles`.

parameters.name: databaseId
parameters.description: PCD database ID to compute profile compatibility for

500.description: Failed to compute compatibility parity map

get.description:
Returns the static feature-by-version-tier compatibility matrix for every
supported Arr application, derived from the authored support ranges. Answers
"which Praxrr features work with which application versions" without needing
a connected instance.

500.description: Failed to build version compatibility matrix

responseTimeMs.description: Database query response time in milliseconds

total.description: Total number of PCD repos configured (verbose only)

cached.description: Number of repos with compiled cache (verbose only)

lastRun.description: Last run time for each job (verbose only)

totalSize.description: Human-readable total size of all backups (verbose only)

retentionDays.description: Configured retention period in days (verbose only)

totalSize.description: Human-readable total size of log files (verbose only)

oldestLog.description: Date of oldest log file (verbose only)

newestLog.description: Date of newest log file (verbose only)

releaseType.description: Release type for series (single_episode, season_pack, etc.)

title.description: Release title to parse and evaluate

cfMatches.description: Map of custom format ID to whether it matches

databaseId.description: Database ID to use for custom format evaluation. If omitted, only parsing is performed (no CF matching).

parserAvailable.description: Whether the parser service is available

title.description: Release title to parse and evaluate

profileNames.description: Quality profile names to score against (max 10)

arrType.description: Arr type for score column resolution

counts.description: Distinct related-entity counts keyed by node kind, plus total

score.description: Score assigned by the quality profile

profiles.description: Quality profile names in this database

hasFile.description: Whether the movie has a downloaded file

qualityName.description: Quality of the downloaded file (null if no file)

fileName.description: File name of the downloaded file (null if no file)

customFormats.description: Custom formats matched on the file

cutoffMet.description: Whether the cutoff score has been met

isPraxrrProfile.description: Whether the profile is managed by Praxrr

sizeOnDisk.description: Total size of episode files in bytes
Total size of all episode files in bytes

isPraxrrProfile.description: Whether the profile is managed by Praxrr

LidarrLibraryItem.description:
Placeholder contract for Lidarr library payload items.
Lidarr-specific artist/album fields remain capability-gated for later phases.

hasFile.description: Whether the episode has a downloaded file

fileName.description: File name of the downloaded file

customFormats.description: Custom formats matched on the file

cutoffMet.description: Whether the cutoff score has been met

totalRecords.description: Total number of records after search/filtering before pagination

totalPages.description: Total page count derived from totalRecords and pageSize

hasNext.description: Indicates whether additional pages are available

totalRecords.description: Total number of records after search/filtering before pagination

totalPages.description: Total page count derived from totalRecords and pageSize

hasNext.description: Indicates whether additional pages are available

totalRecords.description: Total number of records after search/filtering before pagination

totalPages.description: Total page count derived from totalRecords and pageSize

hasNext.description: Indicates whether additional pages are available

LibraryResponse.description:
Library response varies by instance type.
Payload details for partially supported families remain capability-gated until
their dedicated library routes are available.

episodes.description: Episode details with quality and score data (Sonarr-only in current phase)

indexers.description: Indexers where this release was found

rawCount.description: Total number of raw releases before grouping

staleCustomFormats.description: Custom formats that are no longer in sync selections

staleQualityProfiles.description: Quality profiles that are no longer in sync selections

reason.description: Why the item was skipped (e.g. "Profile is assigned to media")

deletedCustomFormats.description: Custom formats that were successfully deleted

deletedQualityProfiles.description: Quality profiles that were successfully deleted

skippedQualityProfiles.description: Quality profiles that were skipped (assigned to media)

sections.description: Optional section filters for preview generation
Optional section filters for preview application

SyncPreviewStatus.description:
Preview lifecycle state:
- `generating`: build in progress
- `ready`: preview fully materialized and runnable
- `applying`: preview is being executed as sync
- `applied`: execution succeeded
- `failed`: generation or execution failed
- `expired`: staleness TTL elapsed

SyncPreviewSection.description: Sync section handled by preview generation

current.description: Previous value from Arr GET state

entityType.description: Sync target entity kind (e.g., customFormat, qualityProfile)

fields.description: Field-level diff; empty for unchanged/create where no deltas are tracked

skipped.description: True when the section had no config to preview and was skipped.

error.description: Section-level preview generation error, when present.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when preview was generated

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when preview becomes expired

error.description: Optional error message from generation or apply

sectionOutcomes.description: Per-section preview generation status used to enforce safe apply behavior.

EntityType.description:
Portable PCD entity identifiers used by import/export endpoints.

Lidarr media-management entities are first-class portable contract members:
- `lidarr_naming`
- `lidarr_media_settings`
- `lidarr_quality_definitions`

Runtime validation is deterministic and fail-fast for Lidarr payloads:
payloads are rejected when required fields are missing, when unsupported
fields are present, or when cross-family fields are mixed into the payload
(for example Radarr naming fields with `lidarr_naming`).

Clients must emit `lidarr_*` entity types for Lidarr entities.

format.description:
Migration payload format marker (`json` for JSON payloads, `yaml` for YAML
in future hybrid ingestion flows).

version.description: Migration schema contract version for hybrid payloads.

PortableMigrationMetadata.description:
Optional metadata for hybrid JSON/YAML migration envelopes.
Existing payloads are backward-compatible when omitted.

data.description:
The portable entity payload. Shape varies by entityType:
- `delay_profile` → PortableDelayProfile
- `regular_expression` → PortableRegularExpression
- `custom_format` → PortableCustomFormat
- `quality_profile` → PortableQualityProfile (contains PortableCustomFormatScore with family-scoped arrType)
- `radarr_naming` → PortableRadarrNaming
- `sonarr_naming` → PortableSonarrNaming
- `lidarr_naming` → PortableLidarrNaming
- `radarr_media_settings` → PortableMediaSettings
- `sonarr_media_settings` → PortableMediaSettings
- `lidarr_media_settings` → PortableLidarrMediaSettings
- `radarr_quality_definitions` → PortableQualityDefinitions
- `sonarr_quality_definitions` → PortableQualityDefinitions
- `lidarr_metadata_profile` → PortableLidarrMetadataProfile
- `lidarr_quality_definitions` → PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions

`lidarr_*` payloads are validated against strict allowlists so mixed
cross-family payloads fail fast during import.

databaseId.description: The PCD database ID to import into

layer.description: The operation layer to write to

data.description:
The portable entity payload (same shape as ExportResponse.data).
For `lidarr_*` entity types, runtime validation rejects mixed
cross-family payload fields and unknown fields.

items.description: Condition data (shape varies by condition type)

arrType.description:
Arr target for this score mapping. `lidarr` is a first-class value
so quality profile custom-format scores can be scoped to a specific Arr
app family. `all` applies the score to every supported family.

Non-regression contract: existing `radarr`, `sonarr`, and `all`
enum semantics are unchanged; `lidarr` is an explicit additional arr
target value and does not introduce fallback alias behavior.

PortableSonarrNaming.description: Portable naming payload for Sonarr entities.

PortableLidarrNaming.description:
Portable naming payload for `lidarr_naming`.
The wire shape matches `PortableSonarrNaming` (shared column structure).
Field semantics are Lidarr-native (artist/album/track tokens).

PortableMediaSettings.description: Shared media-management settings payload shape used across Arr families.

PortableLidarrMediaSettings.description:
Portable payload for `lidarr_media_settings`.
The shape matches `PortableMediaSettings`.

PortableQualityDefinitions.description: Shared quality-definition payload shape used across Arr families.

items.description: Quality definition entry with min/max/preferred sizes

PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions.description:
Portable payload for `lidarr_quality_definitions`.
The shape matches `PortableQualityDefinitions`.

SnapshotType.description: Whether the snapshot was auto-created or user-initiated

SnapshotTrigger.description:
What event triggered the snapshot creation.
- pull: before a PCD repository pull/refresh
- sync: before an Arr instance sync
- manual: user-initiated via API

opsSequenceMaxId.description: Maximum pcd_ops ID at snapshot time

targetInstanceIds.description: Arr instance IDs targeted by this operation

opsWrittenSince.description: Number of ops written after this snapshot (count of pcd_ops rows with id greater than opsSequenceMaxId)

isRestorable.description: Always false in this release. Restore support is not yet implemented.

total.description: Total count matching the query filters

CreateManualSnapshotRequest.description: Request body for creating a manual snapshot. All fields are optional.

completed.description: Whether the wizard has been marked completed

dismissedAt.description: Timestamp the wizard was dismissed, or null if never dismissed

hasArrInstance.description: Whether at least one Arr instance has been connected

hasDatabase.description: Whether at least one PCD database has been linked

hasProfileSelections.description: Whether at least one instance has quality profile selections synced

prerequisites.description: Setup prerequisite progress derived from real entity state

configured.description: Whether PRAXRR_DEFAULT_DB_URL resolves to a non-empty value

url.description: Resolved default database URL, or null when not configured

alreadyLinked.description: Whether any PCD database is already linked

defaultDatabase.description: Default database auto-link info used to render the link-database step

SetupStatePatchRequest.description: Advance the wizard to the given step

appName.description: Arr application name, present on success

version.description: Arr application version, present on success

reason.description: Sanitized failure reason, present on failure

scope.description: Parity entity or workflow surface this semantic difference applies to

apps.description: Arr apps this entry applies to

summary.description: One-line summary of the semantic divergence

detail.description: Full explanation of why the divergence exists

suggestion.description: Suggested alternative or mitigation, when applicable

sourceRefs.description: Prose file/symbol anchors for drift audits, not imports

compatibleArrTypes.description: Arr app types this profile's enabled qualities are compatible with

basis.description: Algorithm basis used to compute this compatibility verdict

entities.description: PCD config entities tracked by the Cross-Arr Parity Map

apps.description: Arr app types represented as matrix columns

label.description: Human-readable label for this entity row

radarr.description: Radarr support status for this entity

sonarr.description: Sonarr support status for this entity

lidarr.description: Lidarr support status for this entity

matrix.description: One row per parity entity, with a per-app support status column

profiles.description: Per-profile Arr-type compatibility, when quality profile data is available

ArrSupportTier.description: Coarse support tier for a detected (arr_type, application version) pair

minimumRecommended.description: Versions below this are degraded (below recommended)

latestTested.description: Latest tested version; newer versions stay supported with an informational warning

eolBelow.description: Versions at or below this are degraded with an end-of-support advisory

breakingAtOrAbove.description: Versions at or above this are unsupported (known or expected breaking major)

degradedFeatures.description: Feature surfaces gated to degraded when the tier is degraded

eolNote.description: Extra advisory copy surfaced with an end-of-support warning

feature.description: Feature surface (workflow or sync) this row describes

tiers.description: Per-tier resolved availability status for this feature

features.description: Per-feature availability across every support tier

ResolvedLayer.description:
PCD configuration layer represented by a resolved-state response:
- `base`: schema + base + tweaks ops only (user ops omitted)
- `user`: user-op overrides relative to the base layer, expressed as a
  field-level diff
- `resolved`: the fully replayed state (schema -> base -> tweaks -> user),
  matching what sync applies to Arr instances

present.description: Whether the entity exists in the requested layer

entity.description:
Canonical Portable payload for the entity in the requested layer.
Present for `layer=base` and `layer=resolved` when `present` is true;
null when the entity is absent from that layer or when `layer=user`.
Shape varies by entityType (and arrType for per-arr-app entity
types):
- `delayProfile` -> PortableDelayProfile
- `regularExpression` -> PortableRegularExpression
- `customFormat` -> PortableCustomFormat
- `qualityProfile` -> PortableQualityProfile
- `naming` (radarr) -> PortableRadarrNaming
- `naming` (sonarr) -> PortableSonarrNaming
- `naming` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrNaming
- `mediaSettings` (radarr, sonarr) -> PortableMediaSettings
- `mediaSettings` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrMediaSettings
- `qualityDefinitions` (radarr, sonarr) -> PortableQualityDefinitions
- `qualityDefinitions` (lidarr) -> PortableLidarrQualityDefinitions
- `lidarrMetadataProfile` -> PortableLidarrMetadataProfile

overrides.description:
Field-level diff of user-op overrides relative to the base layer.
Present only for `layer=user`; an empty array means the resolved
value matches base exactly.

hasPendingConflict.description:
True when a pending value-guard conflict targets this entity (see
`pcd_op_history`). The resolved value must not be treated as
unambiguous while this is true.

entities.description: Per-entity resolved state for the requested layer

arrType.description:
Arr instance family, or null when the instance's own `arr_type` value is
unrecognized (the `incompatible` error case).

compatible.description: Whether this entity type is supported for the instance's arr_type

present.description: Whether the entity is present in this instance's desired payload

desired.description:
Per-instance transformed-desired Portable payload, present when
`compatible` and `present` are both true. Uses the same
entityType/arrType-to-Portable-shape mapping as
`ResolvedEntityState.entity`.

actual.description:
Raw live Arr state for this instance, only populated when
`includeLive=true` and the live fetch succeeded. Shape is the
upstream Arr API payload, not a Portable shape.

error.description:
Sanitized reason when this instance's desired/live lookup failed.
Raw error detail never appears here; full detail is logged
server-side only. Superset of the single-instance live-diff reason
enum: `incompatible` (unrecognized arr_type), `rate-limited`
(per-instance live fetch throttled), and `error` (unexpected
failure, detail logged not surfaced) are comparison-specific.
`not_configured` means the section has no sync configuration on the
instance at all.

instanceId.description: Arr instance ID this diff row belongs to

changes.description:
Field-level changes relative to the first compatible
instance's desired payload.

diffs.description:
Pairwise diffs of each compatible instance's desired payload against
the first compatible instance (the baseline).

instanceId.description: Arr instance ID the live diff was computed against

changes.description: Desired-vs-actual field changes; an empty array means the entity is in sync

GraphNodeKind.description:
PCD entity family a dependency-graph node represents. `quality` and
`quality_definition` are leaf kinds with no per-id editor route (see
`GraphNode.routeId`).

GraphArrType.description:
Arr scope of a dependency edge. `all` is a first-class, distinct value (an
edge that applies to every Arr app); it is never expanded into per-arr
edges and never establishes per-arr quality-profile compatibility.

GraphEdgeKind.description:
Relationship family. Direction is always referrer (`from`) -> dependency
(`to`).

name.description: Name-keyed identifier of the referenced entity

routeId.description:
Integer editor id for linkable kinds (`custom_format`,
`regular_expression`, `quality_profile`); null for leaf kinds
(`quality`, `quality_definition`) that have no per-id editor route.

inDegree.description: Number of edges pointing at this node (how many entities reference it)

outDegree.description: Number of edges leaving this node (how many entities it depends on)

compatibleArrTypes.description:
Arr apps this node is valid for, present only for `quality_profile` and
`quality` nodes. For quality profiles it is derived from
`computeProfileCompatibility` (never from `arr_type='all'` scores).

score.description: Custom-format score, present only on `quality_profile_custom_format` edges

groupName.description: Quality group name, present only on grouped `quality_profile_quality` edges

nodes.description: Every in-scope entity node, with in/out degree and (where applicable) compatibility

edges.description: Referrer -> dependency edges, filtered by the requested arrType/nodeKind

arrTypesPresent.description: Distinct arr scopes present across the returned edges

truncated.description: True when the graph exceeded the server-side edge cap and was truncated

direction.description:
`dependents` (default) = entities that reference this node (reverse
traversal); `dependencies` = entities this node references (forward).

edges.description: All edges discovered by the bounded traversal

byArrType.description: The same edges grouped by arrType (arr scopes never collapsed)

counts.description: Count of distinct related entities keyed by node kind, plus `total`

hasDownstream.description: True when at least one edge was found (there is downstream impact)

truncated.description: True when the traversal hit its depth or visited cap and stopped early
